summary: Drew DeVault announces his FOSDEM 2023 schedule, with four speaking slots covering his major projects. He will give both a lightning talk and full-length talk on Helios, a microkernel written in Hare inspired by seL4. He is also hosting community meetups for both the Hare programming language and SourceHut. The post serves as an invitation to the community and a brief overview of the current state of each project.
key_points: FOSDEM 2023 marks the return to in-person conferences after COVID, and DeVault has an unusually full schedule with four slots
Helios is a microkernel written in Hare, inspired by seL4, intended partly to demonstrate Hare's applicability to kernel development
Hare is positioned as a simple, stable systems language with static types, manual memory management, and a minimal runtime
SourceHut is completing its GraphQL rollout and migrating to the EU, marking significant infrastructure milestones
money_quotes: FOSDEM is right around the corner, and finally in person after long years of dealing with COVID.
Hare is a systems programming language designed to be simple, stable, and robust.
Hare uses a static type system, manual memory management, and a minimal runtime.
Helios is a simple microkernel written in part to demonstrate the applicability of the Hare programming language to kernels.
It is well-suited to writing operating systems, system tools, compilers, networking software, and other low-level, high performance tasks.
key_insight: DeVault's FOSDEM 2023 lineup showcases how his projects form an integrated stack: Hare as the language, Helios as the kernel proving ground, and SourceHut as the development platform.
